subject: Foods and Nutrition

preamble.description: Food is one of the basic needs of man. Without good food, an individual would not be able to function properly in all spheres of life. Good food promotes health and therefore it is important that students are exposed to ways of selecting, preparing and cooking wholesome food for themselves and others. Foods and Nutrition as a vocational subject prepares students for the world of work. It also encourages the creative use of local foods and associated food service to meet the nutritional needs and other demands of consumers.

aims_and_objectives: acquire basic knowledge about foods and nutrition;
understand the relationship between nutrition and health;
identify career opportunities in foods and nutrition and acquire skills for further studies;
apply the general principles underlying meal planning, selection, preparation and serving of food to feed family and other consumers for different occasions;
understand the need for planning an efficient and safe kitchen;
choose, use, care and store kitchen equipment and tools effectively;
appreciate the importance of sanitation in the kitchen food preparation and service;
apply basic principles underlying food processing, storage and preservation;
acquire basic knowledge in consumer education;
acquire research skills and use the information to experiment, develop and improve local dishes;
set up a business in the food industry using all the basic skills acquired.

papers.description: Comprise sixty multiple choice questions, all of which are to be answered within 1 hour for 60 marks.
Comprise six essay questions out of which candidates will be required to answer four. Carries 40 marks and will last 1 hour 15 minutes.
Practical test of 3 hours duration. Carries 100 marks and will be conducted by a visiting examiner.

scheme_of_examination.note: Papers 1 and 2 will be a composite paper and will be taken at one sitting.

topics.content_notes: Meaning; Basic knowledge of nutrition; Importance of human nutrition; Factors affecting foods and nutrition; Food habits.

topics.topic: 2. Careers associated with Foods and Nutrition
topics.content_notes: Careers associated with foods and nutrition; Factors that influence career choices; Characteristics required for foods and nutrition careers.

Nutrients and their nutritive values; Functions of different nutrients; Sources of different nutrients; Dietary deficiencies; Metabolism (digestion and absorption of carbohydrates, proteins, fats); Knowledge and use of food composition tables; Food tests (simple physical and chemical tests of foodstuffs to detect presence of nutrients e.g., proteins, fats, and carbohydrates).
Types of kitchen (traditional, modern, institutional); Planning different types of kitchen; Factors to consider when planning; Cleaning agents and abrasives (commercial and local).
Classes and types of equipment; Selection, use, care, and storage of large and small equipment, fixtures, and appliances.
Types of kitchen accidents; Causes and prevention of accidents; Content and use of first aid box; Simple first aid treatment.

Meal planning terms, reasons, factors, general principles, and meal patterns; Special nutritional needs (pregnant, lactating mothers, infants, children, adolescents, adults, aged, vegetarians); Planning meals for specific health conditions (HIV & AIDS, invalids, convalescents, overweight, underweight, hypertension, diabetes, etc.); Sanitation (personal hygiene, kitchen hygiene, food hygiene including handling, food-borne diseases, food sanitation laws).
Milk and milk products (cheese, yoghurt), eggs, meat, poultry, fish (kinds/types, structure, selection, storage).
Kinds and forms; Importance (versatility, thickening, cheap source of energy/good source of roughage).
Classification, structure, nutritive value, storage, and importance in the diet.
Classification and importance in the diet.
Kinds and classification (vegetable and animal fats/oils); Uses in diet and body; Health implications.
Types of starchy roots, plantain, and dishes.
Meaning, causes, and agents of food spoilage.
Meaning, importance, general principles, and methods of food preservation.
Meaning, importance, storage of perishable and non-perishable foods, storage space and equipment (refrigeration, deep freezer, cupboards, shelves, granaries, barns, etc.).
Explanation of concept and reasons for cooking food.
Moist heat methods (boiling, stewing, steaming); Dry heat methods (baking, roasting, grilling, frying); Effects of heat on different food commodities.
Convection (liquid, gases), radiation (space), conduction (solid metal).
Explanation of cooking terms.
Definitions, types, functions, selection and use, food adulteration.

Types of flour, basic ingredients, raising agents (steam, carbon dioxide, air), batters and doughs, biscuits classification, cakes (rubbed in, creamed, whisked, melted, sponge mixtures), pastries (shortcrust, suet, flaky, etc.), yeast mixtures, cake decoration/icing.

topics.topic: Beverages classification, importance, and preparation principles
topics.content_notes: Non-alcoholic (coffee, tea, cocoa, milk/egg drinks, fruit juices/drinks, beef tea, lemon grass) and alcoholic (beer, wine); Nutritive values.
Types, selection, uses, advantages, disadvantages, and preparation of dishes using convenience foods.
Explanation, importance, rules/guidelines, and rechauffé dishes.
Explanation, occasions, types, and preparation of festive/special occasion dishes.

topics.topic: Entertainment, Table setting, and Meal Service
topics.content_notes: Explanation, types of parties, factors for success; table setting, cover, appointments, manners/etiquette; meal service styles (informal, formal) and factors determining choice.

topics.topic: Research and experiments in local dishes
topics.content_notes: Importance of research, data collection, developing/improving local dishes, recipes, nutritive value, time/energy use; experiments using fruits (passion fruit, samia, royal palm fruit) and alternative flours (root, cereal, legume).
Meaning, principles, and consumer agents; Food budgeting factors; Food purchasing guidelines, bulk purchasing reasons, advantages, and disadvantages.

topics.topic: Setting up a catering enterprise and work ethics
topics.content_notes: Explanation of terms (entrepreneur, enterprise); characteristics, pros and cons; setting up and success factors; work ethics; food packaging (reasons, qualities, types of materials).
